regions.us-ports: Today Miami experiences east winds at 8 knots with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, allowing routine embarkation and shore excursion operations to proceed without weather delays. Heat Advisory in effect for Port Everglades (heat index up to 109°F), Manhattan and Brooklyn (heat index up to 104°F), Bayonne (heat index up to 101°F), Boston (heat index up to 101°F), Los Angeles and Long Beach (temperature up to 96°F), and San Diego (temperature up to 90°F); Extreme Heat Warning in effect for Philadelphia (heat index up to 107°F). Through the next 48 hours and beyond, east-southeast winds strengthen gradually to 9 knots on Day 2 and southeast winds reach 11 knots by Day 3, with overcast conditions persisting and less than 10% rain probability maintained across all three days, presenting stable conditions for port operations throughout the forecast period.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today elevated heat with index values reaching 100 degrees poses a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions across Nassau, Freeport, Key West, Grand Cayman, and the Dominican ports, with easterly winds at 10 knots and overcast skies limiting relief; the next 24 to 48 hours will see heat index climbing to 101 degrees under ESE winds near 7 knots with continued overcast conditions and less than 10% rain probability affecting all cruise ports from the Bahamas through the eastern Caribbean.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today a Heat Advisory is in effect for San Juan with heat index values reaching 104 degrees, creating elevated risk for passengers during outdoor shore excursions; meanwhile east winds at 17 knots with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability support normal port operations and embarkation. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, east winds holding at 16 knots with drizzle and less than 10% rain probability will allow continued vessel operations across San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, and Antigua with minimal weather impact to scheduled activities.
regions.western-caribbean: Today easterly winds at 12 knots with thunderstorms and 27% rain probability will allow port operations and most shore excursions to proceed normally in Cozumel, Costa Maya, Roatan, and Belize City, though isolated showers may briefly interrupt outdoor activities. Tomorrow thunderstorms with 40% rain probability may affect all four ports as ENE winds hold at 10 knots, requiring flexible scheduling for water-based excursions. By day three, ENE winds at 11 knots with thunderstorms and 34% rain probability may impact Cozumel and Costa Maya most notably, while conditions gradually stabilize thereafter.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today elevated easterly winds of 21 knots are affecting Aruba with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, creating moderate sea conditions that may delay tender operations and require caution during water-based shore excursions. Over the next 48 hours, winds strengthen to 22 knots on day two across the Southern Caribbean with drizzle and 10% rain probability at Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena, posing operational challenges for port tendering and outdoor activities. Beyond 48 hours, easterly winds ease slightly to 21 knots on day three with less than 10% rain probability, though drizzle conditions persist across the region.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today across the Lesser Antilles, ENE winds at 12 knots with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability will support normal port operations and shore excursions at Barbados, St. Lucia, Martinique, Dominica, Antigua, St. Kitts, St. Maarten, St. Vincent, and Grenada. Over the next 48 hours, easterly winds remain steady at 12 knots with drizzle developing; the 10% rain probability on Day 2 and 14% rain probability on Day 3 will not materially disrupt embarkation, disembarkation, or excursion scheduling across the region. Beyond 48 hours, the easterly flow persists with drizzle and light rain probabilities remaining below 15%, indicating stable conditions for cruise operations through the forecast period.
regions.eastern-pacific: Today elevated heat with index values reaching 101F poses a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions across Cabo San Lucas, Ensenada, Huatulco, Manzanillo, Mazatlan, and Puerto Vallarta, with S winds at 10kt and overcast skies limiting cloud cover relief. Day 2 brings excessive heat with index values reaching 105F alongside W winds at 10kt and drizzle conditions that may affect port operations and tendering at all six ports, with 12% rain probability. Beyond 48 hours, WNW winds at 11kt will persist through Day 3 with drizzle and 17% rain probability, though conditions remain marginal for most cruise activities across the region.
regions.los-angeles: Today westerly winds at 12 knots with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability will support normal embarkation and disembarkation operations across Los Angeles, Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island. Over the next 24-48 hours, southwesterly winds at 11 knots will persist while fog develops on Day 2, though less than 10% rain probability means shore excursions will proceed without precipitation concerns. Beyond 48 hours, southeasterly winds at 10 knots will accompany increasing cloud cover on Day 3, maintaining the less than 10% rain probability trend across all four ports.
regions.ensenada: Today light southerly winds at 1 knot with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability will allow smooth port operations and unrestricted tender access at Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, westerly-southwest winds will increase to 8 to 9 knots with persistent fog developing across all three ports, reducing visibility but maintaining less than 10% rain probability and allowing continued shore excursion operations. Beyond 48 hours, westerly-southwest winds hold steady at 8 knots as fog clears to reveal clear skies by day three, sustaining less than 10% rain probability throughout the extended forecast period.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today elevated heat with a heat index reaching 101F poses a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions at Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz, with south winds at 10 knots and overcast skies limiting sun relief. Tomorrow an excessive heat day with heat index reaching 105F and westerly 10-knot winds accompanied by drizzle with 12% rain probability will require extended hydration protocols for port operations and excursion activities across all three ports. Beyond 48 hours, westnorthwesterly winds at 11 knots and drizzle with 17% rain probability suggest slight moderation in heat stress but continued moisture and operational caution through day three.
regions.mazatlan: Today thunderstorms with 48% rain probability may affect shore excursions across Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata, with heat index values reaching 102 degrees posing serious risk to passengers during outdoor activities; NW winds at 8 knots will provide minimal relief from conditions. Day 2 brings intensifying thunderstorm activity with 62% rain probability expected to impact port operations, accompanied by WNW winds at 6 knots and heat index reaching 102 degrees. By Day 3, thunderstorm chances diminish to 41% rain probability with WNW winds at 6 knots and heat index declining to 100 degrees, though elevated heat persists across all three ports.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today northeast winds at 7 knots accompany partly cloudy skies with thunderstorms and a 40% rain probability that may affect shore excursions throughout Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala, while heat index values reaching 104 degrees pose a serious risk to passengers during outdoor activities. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, east winds at 8 knots persist with continued thunderstorms and a 37% rain probability that may impact port operations and tender services. Beyond 48 hours, southwesterly winds at 8 knots accompany diminishing thunderstorm activity with a 32% rain probability and heat index values near 100 degrees as conditions gradually stabilize.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today southeastern winds at 8 knots with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability will allow smooth port operations and outdoor shore excursions across Barcelona, Valencia, Palma de Mallorca, Ibiza, Malaga, Cadiz, Lisbon, and Gibraltar. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, southwesterly winds increase to 11 knots under continued overcast conditions with less than 10% rain probability, maintaining favorable embarkation and disembarkation windows at all ports. Beyond 48 hours, easterly winds shift to 9 knots as fog develops across the region, reducing visibility during Day 3 operations but with less than 10% rain probability persisting.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today elevated heat with heat index reaching 101F poses a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions across the region, particularly in Naples, Civitavecchia, and Sardinia, with SSW winds at 8 knots and clear skies supporting port operations. Tomorrow through day three, heat index values remain elevated at 100F and 101F respectively as SW winds hold 7-8 knots with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, maintaining stable conditions for embarkation, disembarkation, and excursion planning at all Central Mediterranean ports.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today north-northeasterly winds at 9 knots with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability will support normal port operations and outdoor shore excursions across Athens, Santorini, Mykonos, Rhodes, Corfu, Istanbul, Izmir, Cyprus, Haifa, and Alexandria. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, heat index values are forecast to reach 100 degrees Fahrenheit, particularly affecting passengers during daytime excursions in Rhodes, Cyprus, and Haifa, while east-northeasterly winds remain light at 7 knots with continued less than 10% rain probability.
regions.southeast-alaska: Today the Juneau area will see WNW winds at 6 knots with drizzle and less than 10% rain probability, supporting normal port operations and allowing most shore excursions to proceed as scheduled. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, conditions will shift to NW winds at 3 knots on Day 2 with drizzle and a 22% rain probability that may affect outdoor activities in Juneau and nearby ports, though operations will remain viable. By Day 3, westerly winds at 5 knots and overcast skies with less than 10% rain probability indicate improving stability for cruise vessel movements and passenger activities throughout the region.
